body.title: _pe_escalate HIGH-inertia branch — verify target_file exists before proposing
body.result: Added file-existence precondition to _pe_escalate HIGH-inertia branch (pe_chain.py:2082-2094). If target_file doesn't resolve under _REPO_ROOT, rewrites reason to 'hallucinated file: <path>' and falls through to the block branch (cc_queue.py block, not propose). Updated test_scope_guard kernel.py refs → core_patterns.py. 2 new tests + 2 updated. 139 tests pass. Commit e3c41813.
body.description:
Observed: Igor emits '[DESIGN PROPOSAL] ...: Igor wants to edit wild_igor/igor/brainstem/kernel.py (HIGH inertia)' for a file that does not exist anywhere in the repo (confirmed via find ~ and git log --all). The proposal reaches CC as a HIGH-inertia approval request, masking the real root cause (hallucinated file path).

The file-existence check SHOULD be enforced at HYPOTHESIZE validation (pe_chain.py:1408-1410), but proposals for nonexistent files still reach the channel — meaning some path bypasses validation, OR validation passes on a path that resolves to something unexpected. Either way, the DESIGN_PROPOSAL branch in _pe_escalate should be the belt-and-suspenders backstop.

Fix: in pe_chain.py:2076-2094 (the 'is_high_inertia and ticket_id' branch of _pe_escalate), add a precondition check: before posting the DESIGN PROPOSAL, verify (_REPO_ROOT / target_file).exists(). If not, post a diagnostic message instead ('[pe_chain] ✗ {ticket_id}: tier2 proposed nonexistent file {target_file} — hallucination, blocking without CC escalation'), block the ticket with reason 'hallucinated file: <path>', and return.

Rationale: asking CC to approve editing a nonexistent file is noise, not a real decision. Better diagnostic = faster root-cause identification of any future recurrence. Companion defense to T-situate-kernel-hallucination-fix (which cuts off the SITUATE source of hallucinations); this ticket catches the same class of bug if it enters via HYPOTHESIZE or any other path.

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/tools/pe_chain.py (_pe_escalate, lines ~2076-2094), tests/test_pe_entry_nodes.py (new test).
**Design rules:** docs-in-code (_pe_escalate docstring should describe the guard), test-plan-or-why-not (test required).
**Scope boundary:** IN — _pe_escalate HIGH-inertia branch, file-existence precondition, channel message shape. OUT — rearchitecting escalation, adding new reason kinds, changing MEDIUM-inertia branch.
**Test plan:** (a) test _pe_escalate with target_file='wild_igor/igor/brainstem/kernel.py' (nonexistent) and HIGH-inertia reason posts the hallucination-diagnostic message and does NOT post DESIGN PROPOSAL; (b) test _pe_escalate with a real HIGH-inertia file (e.g. core_patterns.py) still posts the DESIGN PROPOSAL normally.
